title: MarginalizationPreserves
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- claim_3_16 part 1/4 (LN remark item 1): for any CDMG `G` and any
set `W`, marginalization preserves ancestral relations on vertices
outside `W`. For `v₁, v₂ ∉ W`, `v₁` is an ancestor of `v₂` in `G` iff
`v₁` is an ancestor of `v₂` in the marginalization `G.marginalize W`.

The implicit `v₁ ∈ G` precondition of the LN's "$v_1, v_2 \in G$"
preamble is already carried by the `mem_Anc` membership on each side
(both `G.Anc v₂` and `(G.marginalize W).Anc v₂` are set-builders
guarded by `_ ∈ G` resp. `_ ∈ G.marginalize W`); the explicit
`v_i ∉ W` hypotheses bridge the two memberships. The proof (to be
filled in by `prove_claim_in_lean`) shuttles a directed walk through
`W` in `G` to / from a single directed edge in `G^{\sm W}` via
`mem_marginalize_E`. -/
theorem marginalize_anc_iff (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) {v₁ v₂ : α}
    (h₁ : v₁ ∉ W) (h₂ : v₂ ∉ W) :
    v₁ ∈ G.Anc v₂ ↔ v₁ ∈ (G.marginalize W).Anc v₂ := by
  -- v₁ ∈ G.Anc v₂ ↔ v₁ ∈ G ∧ ∃ π : Walk G v₁ v₂, π.IsDirected.
  -- v₁ ∈ G ↔ v₁ ∈ G.marginalize W given v₁ ∉ W (both reduce to G.J ∪ (G.V \ W)).
  -- Walk existence transports via the two translators.
  rw [CDMG.mem_Anc, CDMG.mem_Anc]
  -- Membership equivalence: v₁ ∈ G ↔ v₁ ∈ G.marginalize W given v₁ ∉ W.
  have h_mem_iff : v₁ ∈ G ↔ v₁ ∈ G.marginalize W := by
    rw [CDMG.mem_iff, CDMG.mem_iff, CDMG.marginalize_J, CDMG.marginalize_V]
    constructor
    · rintro (hJ | hV)
      · exact Or.inl hJ
      · exact Or.inr ⟨hV, h₁⟩
    · rintro (hJ | ⟨hV, _⟩)
      · exact Or.inl hJ
      · exact Or.inr hV
  refine ⟨fun ⟨h_in_G, π, hπ_dir⟩ => ⟨h_mem_iff.mp h_in_G, ?_⟩,
          fun ⟨h_in_marg, π', hπ'_dir⟩ => ⟨h_mem_iff.mpr h_in_marg, ?_⟩⟩
  · -- (→) direction: shrink the directed walk in G to a directed walk in marginalize.
    obtain ⟨π', hπ'_dir, _, _, _⟩ := Walk.exists_marg_directed_of_directed W π hπ_dir h₁ h₂
    exact ⟨π', hπ'_dir⟩
  · -- (←) direction: expand the directed walk in marginalize to one in G.
    obtain ⟨ρ, hρ_dir, _, _, _, _⟩ := Walk.exists_directed_of_marg_directed W π' hπ'_dir
    exact ⟨ρ, hρ_dir⟩

/-- claim_3_16 part 2/4 (LN remark item 2, no-source half): for any
CDMG `G` and any set `W`, marginalization preserves the existence of
bifurcations between two vertices `v₁, v₂ ∈ G \ W`. The LN's "between"
is read symmetrically in `v₁, v₂` and encoded as a disjunction over
the two walk directions; the disjunction shape is also what makes the
biconditional handle the `L^{\sm W}` exclusion clause cleanly (a bidir
hinge in `G` absorbed by the exclusion still gives a `.backward`-hinge
bifurcation in `G^{\sm W}` *in the opposite walk direction*).

The with-source variant ("with source `v_3`") is intentionally
*deferred* to a follow-up dispatch — see the file docstring and risk
§5.2 in `workspace_claim_3_16.md`. The proof (to be filled in by
`prove_claim_in_lean`) shuttles a bifurcation's arms and hinge through
`W` via `mem_marginalize_E` (for the directed arms) and
`mem_marginalize_L` (for the bidir hinge), with the symmetric `∨`
absorbing the exclusion-clause case-split. -/
theorem marginalize_bifurcation_iff (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    {v₁ v₂ : α} (h₁v : v₁ ∈ G) (h₂v : v₂ ∈ G)
    (h₁W : v₁ ∉ W) (h₂W : v₂ ∉ W) :
    ((∃ π : Walk G v₁ v₂, π.IsBifurcation) ∨
     (∃ π : Walk G v₂ v₁, π.IsBifurcation)) ↔
    ((∃ π : Walk (G.marginalize W) v₁ v₂, π.IsBifurcation) ∨
     (∃ π : Walk (G.marginalize W) v₂ v₁, π.IsBifurcation)) := by
  -- A single helper handles ONE walk-direction case in each direction of the iff.
  -- The disjunction structure on both sides matches naturally.
  -- For one direction at a time, we case-split on bifurcation hinge type.
  -- Below, `back_to_marg_bif` handles (←) for a fixed walk direction; we apply
  -- it twice (once per disjunct on the RHS) to discharge (←). The (→) direction
  -- is more involved and handled inline.
  constructor
  · -- (→) direction: G bifurcation → marg bifurcation.
    -- Split on which walk direction the G bifurcation is in.
    rintro (⟨π, hb⟩ | ⟨π, hb⟩)
    · -- G bif in v₁ → v₂ direction.
      rcases Walk.marginalize_bif_forward h₁v h₂v h₁W h₂W π hb with
        ⟨π', hπ', _⟩ | ⟨π', hπ', _⟩
      · exact Or.inl ⟨π', hπ'⟩
      · exact Or.inr ⟨π', hπ'⟩
    · -- G bif in v₂ → v₁ direction (symmetric).
      rcases Walk.marginalize_bif_forward h₂v h₁v h₂W h₁W π hb with
        ⟨π', hπ', _⟩ | ⟨π', hπ', _⟩
      · exact Or.inr ⟨π', hπ'⟩
      · exact Or.inl ⟨π', hπ'⟩
  · -- (←) direction: marg bifurcation → G bifurcation.
    rintro (⟨π', hb'⟩ | ⟨π', hb'⟩)
    · -- marg bif in v₁ → v₂ direction.
      -- marginalize_bif_backward returns σ with IsBifurcation ∧ support tracking;
      -- strip the support tracking for the plain bifurcation iff conclusion.
      rcases Walk.marginalize_bif_backward h₁v h₂v h₁W h₂W π' hb' with
        ⟨σ, hσ, _⟩ | ⟨σ, hσ, _⟩
      · exact Or.inl ⟨σ, hσ⟩
      · exact Or.inr ⟨σ, hσ⟩
    · -- marg bif in v₂ → v₁ direction (symmetric).
      rcases Walk.marginalize_bif_backward h₂v h₁v h₂W h₁W π' hb' with
        ⟨σ, hσ, _⟩ | ⟨σ, hσ, _⟩
      · exact Or.inr ⟨σ, hσ⟩
      · exact Or.inl ⟨σ, hσ⟩

/-- claim_3_16 part 3/4 (LN remark item 3a): for any CDMG `G` and any
set `W`, if `G` is acyclic then so is the marginalization
`G.marginalize W`.

The mathematical content is that every directed edge of `G.marginalize
W` shortcuts a length-`≥ 1` directed walk through `W` in `G` (by
`mem_marginalize_E`), so a non-trivial directed cycle in
`G.marginalize W` expands to a non-trivial directed cycle in `G`,
contradicting `G.IsAcyclic`. This route is independent of claim_3_2
(no topological-order extraction); the topological-order
preservation half is the sibling theorem
`marginalize_isTopologicalOrder` below.

The proof will be filled in by `prove_claim_in_lean`; this dispatch
covers the statement only. -/
theorem marginalize_isAcyclic (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    (h : G.IsAcyclic) : (G.marginalize W).IsAcyclic := by
  -- Contradiction: a directed cycle in G.marginalize W expands to a directed
  -- cycle in G (each shortcut edge expands to a walk of length ≥ 1), contradicting
  -- G.IsAcyclic.
  intro v hv ⟨π', hπ'_dir, hπ'_pos⟩
  obtain ⟨ρ, hρ_dir, hρ_len, _, _, _⟩ :=
    Walk.exists_directed_of_marg_directed W π' hπ'_dir
  have hρ_pos : 1 ≤ ρ.length := by omega
  -- v ∈ G.marginalize W ⇒ v ∈ G.
  have hv_G : v ∈ G := Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hv
  exact h v hv_G ⟨ρ, hρ_dir, hρ_pos⟩

/-- claim_3_16 part 4/4 (LN remark item 3b): for any CDMG `G` and any
set `W`, a topological order `r` of `G` is also a topological order of
the marginalization `G.marginalize W`. The same relation `r` carries
through — the LN's "by just ignoring the nodes from `W`" — because
`IsTopologicalOrder` quantifies its fields over the (smaller) vertex
set of `G.marginalize W`.

The four `IsTopologicalOrder` fields (irreflexivity, transitivity,
trichotomy, parent-precedence) all reduce field-by-field to the
corresponding statement on `G`: the first three by restriction along
`v ∈ G.marginalize W → v ∈ G`, the fourth by chaining `r` along the
directed walk through `W` that underlies each `G.marginalize W` edge
(via `mem_marginalize_E`). See `marginalize_isAcyclic` above for the
acyclicity preservation half of the LN remark.

The proof will be filled in by `prove_claim_in_lean`; this dispatch
covers the statement only. -/
theorem marginalize_isTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    {r : α → α → Prop} (hr : G.IsTopologicalOrder r) :
    (G.marginalize W).IsTopologicalOrder r := by
  -- The four IsTopologicalOrder fields transport: the first three by restriction
  -- (v ∈ G.marginalize W ⇒ v ∈ G), the fourth by chaining r along the directed
  -- walk underlying each marginalize edge via mem_marginalize_E.
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- irrefl: r restricted to G.marginalize W vertices is irreflexive.
    intro v hv
    exact hr.irrefl v (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hv)
  · -- trans: r restricted to G.marginalize W is transitive.
    intro v hv w hw x hx
    exact hr.trans v (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hv) w (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hw)
      x (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hx)
  · -- trichotomous.
    intro v hv w hw
    exact hr.trichotomous v (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hv) w (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hw)
  · -- parent_lt: v ∈ Pa^{G.marginalize W}(w) → r v w.
    -- v ∈ Pa requires (v, w) ∈ (G.marginalize W).E, which by mem_marginalize_E
    -- supplies a length-≥-1 directed walk in G with interior in W; chain via
    -- marg_walk_lt_of_isTopologicalOrder.
    intro v w h_in_Pa
    obtain ⟨_, h_E⟩ := h_in_Pa
    change (v, w) ∈ (G.marginalize W).E at h_E
    rw [CDMG.mem_marginalize_E] at h_E
    obtain ⟨_, _, π, hπ_dir, _, hπ_pos⟩ := h_E
    exact Walk.marg_walk_lt_of_isTopologicalOrder hr π hπ_dir hπ_pos
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem marginalize_anc_iff (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) {v₁ v₂ : α}
    (h₁ : v₁ ∉ W) (h₂ : v₂ ∉ W) :
    v₁ ∈ G.Anc v₂ ↔ v₁ ∈ (G.marginalize W).Anc v₂ := by

  rw [CDMG.mem_Anc, CDMG.mem_Anc]

  have h_mem_iff : v₁ ∈ G ↔ v₁ ∈ G.marginalize W := by
    rw [CDMG.mem_iff, CDMG.mem_iff, CDMG.marginalize_J, CDMG.marginalize_V]
    constructor
    · rintro (hJ | hV)
      · exact Or.inl hJ
      · exact Or.inr ⟨hV, h₁⟩
    · rintro (hJ | ⟨hV, _⟩)
      · exact Or.inl hJ
      · exact Or.inr hV
  refine ⟨fun ⟨h_in_G, π, hπ_dir⟩ => ⟨h_mem_iff.mp h_in_G, ?_⟩,
          fun ⟨h_in_marg, π', hπ'_dir⟩ => ⟨h_mem_iff.mpr h_in_marg, ?_⟩⟩
  ·
    obtain ⟨π', hπ'_dir, _, _, _⟩ := Walk.exists_marg_directed_of_directed W π hπ_dir h₁ h₂
    exact ⟨π', hπ'_dir⟩
  ·
    obtain ⟨ρ, hρ_dir, _, _, _, _⟩ := Walk.exists_directed_of_marg_directed W π' hπ'_dir
    exact ⟨ρ, hρ_dir⟩

theorem marginalize_bifurcation_iff (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    {v₁ v₂ : α} (h₁v : v₁ ∈ G) (h₂v : v₂ ∈ G)
    (h₁W : v₁ ∉ W) (h₂W : v₂ ∉ W) :
    ((∃ π : Walk G v₁ v₂, π.IsBifurcation) ∨
     (∃ π : Walk G v₂ v₁, π.IsBifurcation)) ↔
    ((∃ π : Walk (G.marginalize W) v₁ v₂, π.IsBifurcation) ∨
     (∃ π : Walk (G.marginalize W) v₂ v₁, π.IsBifurcation)) := by

  constructor
  ·

    rintro (⟨π, hb⟩ | ⟨π, hb⟩)
    ·
      rcases Walk.marginalize_bif_forward h₁v h₂v h₁W h₂W π hb with
        ⟨π', hπ', _⟩ | ⟨π', hπ', _⟩
      · exact Or.inl ⟨π', hπ'⟩
      · exact Or.inr ⟨π', hπ'⟩
    ·
      rcases Walk.marginalize_bif_forward h₂v h₁v h₂W h₁W π hb with
        ⟨π', hπ', _⟩ | ⟨π', hπ', _⟩
      · exact Or.inr ⟨π', hπ'⟩
      · exact Or.inl ⟨π', hπ'⟩
  ·
    rintro (⟨π', hb'⟩ | ⟨π', hb'⟩)
    ·

      rcases Walk.marginalize_bif_backward h₁v h₂v h₁W h₂W π' hb' with
        ⟨σ, hσ, _⟩ | ⟨σ, hσ, _⟩
      · exact Or.inl ⟨σ, hσ⟩
      · exact Or.inr ⟨σ, hσ⟩
    ·
      rcases Walk.marginalize_bif_backward h₂v h₁v h₂W h₁W π' hb' with
        ⟨σ, hσ, _⟩ | ⟨σ, hσ, _⟩
      · exact Or.inr ⟨σ, hσ⟩
      · exact Or.inl ⟨σ, hσ⟩

theorem marginalize_isAcyclic (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    (h : G.IsAcyclic) : (G.marginalize W).IsAcyclic := by

  intro v hv ⟨π', hπ'_dir, hπ'_pos⟩
  obtain ⟨ρ, hρ_dir, hρ_len, _, _, _⟩ :=
    Walk.exists_directed_of_marg_directed W π' hπ'_dir
  have hρ_pos : 1 ≤ ρ.length := by omega

  have hv_G : v ∈ G := Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hv
  exact h v hv_G ⟨ρ, hρ_dir, hρ_pos⟩

theorem marginalize_isTopologicalOrder (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    {r : α → α → Prop} (hr : G.IsTopologicalOrder r) :
    (G.marginalize W).IsTopologicalOrder r := by

  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  ·
    intro v hv
    exact hr.irrefl v (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hv)
  ·
    intro v hv w hw x hx
    exact hr.trans v (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hv) w (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hw)
      x (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hx)
  ·
    intro v hv w hw
    exact hr.trichotomous v (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hv) w (Walk.marg_mem_of_marg hw)
  ·

    intro v w h_in_Pa
    obtain ⟨_, h_E⟩ := h_in_Pa
    change (v, w) ∈ (G.marginalize W).E at h_E
    rw [CDMG.mem_marginalize_E] at h_E
    obtain ⟨_, _, π, hπ_dir, _, hπ_pos⟩ := h_E
    exact Walk.marg_walk_lt_of_isTopologicalOrder hr π hπ_dir hπ_pos
lean_explanation:
The lecture-notes remark bundles three preservation properties of CDMG marginalization $G \mapsto G^{\setminus W}$ -- ancestors, bifurcations, and acyclicity together with topological orders. The Lean encoding splits this into four sibling theorems in a single file, all parameterised over a CDMG `G : CDMG α` and a set `W : Set α` of vertices to absorb. Here `α : Type*` is the ambient vertex type (matching the LN's silent assumption that the carrier is fixed once and for all), `G` corresponds to the LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$, and `W` is a fully general `Set α`: the operator `G.marginalize` is defined for *any* set, so neither `W \subseteq G.V` nor finiteness is required as a hypothesis.

`marginalize_anc_iff` takes two vertices `v₁, v₂ : α` plus hypotheses `h₁ : v₁ ∉ W` and `h₂ : v₂ ∉ W`, and concludes the biconditional `v₁ ∈ G.Anc v₂ ↔ v₁ ∈ (G.marginalize W).Anc v₂`. The LN's `$v_1, v_2 \in G$` preamble is implicit: `G.Anc v₂` unfolds to a set-builder of the form `{w | w ∈ G ∧ ∃ π : Walk G w v₂, π.IsDirected}`, so the membership clause is built into the iff's two sides syntactically and need not be quoted.

`marginalize_bifurcation_iff` takes the four hypotheses `v₁ ∈ G`, `v₂ ∈ G`, `v₁ ∉ W`, `v₂ ∉ W` and concludes the iff between a *disjunction-of-walk-directions* on each side: $\bigl(\exists\,\pi : \mathrm{Walk}\,G\,v_1\,v_2,\ \pi.\mathrm{IsBifurcation}\bigr) \vee \bigl(\exists\,\pi : \mathrm{Walk}\,G\,v_2\,v_1,\ \pi.\mathrm{IsBifurcation}\bigr)$, and the same disjunction with `G` replaced by `G.marginalize W`. The disjunction encodes the LN's symmetric reading of "there is a bifurcation between $v_1$ and $v_2$"; a `Walk` in Lean is directional and `IsBifurcation` is *not* symmetric under reversal (the leftArm / rightArm split is direction-aware), so a single existential would not faithfully capture "between".

`marginalize_isAcyclic` is the implication `G.IsAcyclic → (G.marginalize W).IsAcyclic`, and `marginalize_isTopologicalOrder` takes a relation `r : α → α → Prop` (kept *implicit* since it is unifiable from `hr`) plus `hr : G.IsTopologicalOrder r` and concludes `(G.marginalize W).IsTopologicalOrder r` -- the *same* relation `r` carries through unchanged, no re-extraction by classical choice. The bulk of all four proofs reduces to two private walk translators living earlier in the file: a *shrink* that collapses a `G`-walk with interior in `W` to a marginalisation-walk, and an *expand* that unfolds each marginalisation edge back into a `G`-walk through `W`.
design_choices:
**Four theorems instead of one bundled conjunction.** The LN remark packs three preservation properties under one banner, but bundling them into a single `Prop` would force every downstream consumer to destructure or carry irrelevant hypotheses. Different downstream rows quote different sub-items: claim_3_17 cites ancestors and bifurcations to assemble iterated marginalisations, the chapter-4 do-calculus arguments cite acyclicity only, and chapter 5 cites the topological order itself. The four-way split lets each call site quote exactly what it needs.

**Item 2 is restricted to the no-source half; the with-source biconditional is deferred.** The LN parenthetically adds "(with source $v_3$)", but the with-source biconditional interacts non-trivially with how the marginalisation handles bidirected edges. The set $L^{\setminus W}$ of bidirected edges in the marginalisation has an exclusion clause forbidding pairs that are already realised by a directed walk through $W$ in either direction; when this exclusion fires on a bidirected hinge of a bifurcation, the absorbing shortcut is a *directed* edge in $G^{\setminus W}$, and the resulting bifurcation is a backward-hinge bifurcation in the *opposite* walk direction. The symmetric $\vee$-reading of "between" absorbs this exchange cleanly (the reverse-direction bifurcation lives in the second disjunct of the conclusion). The with-source biconditional cannot absorb it cleanly -- a no-source LHS bifurcation in $G$ would relate to a with-source RHS bifurcation in $G^{\setminus W}$, which is not what a with-source predicate reads as on both sides. The with-source variant is therefore deferred until the no-source proof has surfaced this friction concretely.

**Item 3 is split into acyclicity and topological-order theorems.** The LN's sentence "if $G$ is acyclic then so is $G^{\setminus W}$ and a topological order of $G$ induces a topological order on $G^{\setminus W}$" reads as one fact, but the two halves are logically independent. Acyclicity is provable directly by walk concatenation -- every marginalisation edge expands to a $G$-walk of length $\geq 1$, so a cycle in the marginalisation expands to a cycle in $G$ -- without invoking the "acyclic iff has a topological order" equivalence (claim_3_2). Splitting the two avoids an apparent circular dependency and matches downstream needs: some callers want acyclicity preservation without a named order, others want the order itself.

**The same relation `r` carries through for the topological-order half.** The alternative -- proving "*some* topological order exists" via the acyclicity theorem plus claim_3_2 -- would force the order to be re-extracted by `Classical.choice` and would not match the LN's *constructive* phrasing "a topological order of $G$ induces a topological order on $G^{\setminus W}$". Carrying `r` through unchanged is the literal Lean reading of the LN's "by just ignoring the nodes from $W$".

**Preconditions are quoted asymmetrically across the four theorems.** `marginalize_anc_iff` takes only `v_i ∉ W` because membership `_ ∈ G` is already embedded in the `Anc` set-builder on each side; `marginalize_bifurcation_iff` takes both `v_i ∈ G` and `v_i ∉ W` because `IsBifurcation`'s existential does not embed `_ ∈ G` syntactically; the two structural theorems take no vertex hypothesis at all because `IsAcyclic` and `IsTopologicalOrder` are themselves $\forall v \in G$ statements. We use `v_i ∉ W` rather than `v_i ∈ G.marginalize W`: the latter would be a strictly weaker hypothesis when $W \cap J \neq \emptyset$ (input nodes survive marginalisation regardless of `W`-membership), and the LN literally writes "$\notin W$".

**Naming uses the `marginalize_*` prefix throughout.** Sibling rows in this chapter follow a `<conclusion>_<construction>` convention (e.g. `isAcyclic_nodeSplittingOn`); this file deliberately deviates because all four theorems study a single operation, and a shared prefix groups them visually. The dot-notation reading `G.marginalize_isAcyclic W h` then parallels `G.marginalize_J W` and the other projection names from the marginalisation operator's own file.
